titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Guy Serge Metraux Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by John Mikalavicius and Francis Radvonovsky

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday February 11, 2020 at 08:02
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.abstract: The papers consist chiefly of correspondence, reports and printed matter related to Metraux's activities during World War II as a representative of the International Red Cross inspecting prisoner of war camps in the United States. Also included is a small amount of correspondence on his proposed Ph.D. dissertation at Yale University on James G. Huneker. An important correspondent is H. L. Mencken with 17 letters (1943-1944).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Guy Serge Metraux Papers (MS 23). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in five series: I. Personal Papers. II. Official Papers. III. Prisoner of War Camps. IV. Printed Materials. V. Miscellaneous.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of Guy Serge Metraux relate primarily to his service as a delegate of the International Committee of the Red Cross, Delegation to the United States of America, 1944-1946. During this time Metraux visited 190 prisoner of war camps in the Mid-West, South and West and reported on conditions for the Red Cross. A general report of his activities and observations is found in folder 14, "Rapport General et Observations sur les Camps de Prisonniers de Guerre et sur la Delegation aux Etats-Unis, Septembre 1944-Juin 1946," Washington, D.C., June 12, 1946. Series II of the papers includes instructions and memos of the Red Cross, minutes of meetings and Metraux's correspondence and reports. Series III, the largest part of the papers, consists of materials prepared and collected at individual camps arranged alphabetically by the name of the camp.

In addition to the papers relating to American prisoner of war camps, there is a group of papers itn Series I relating to Mr. Metraux's proposed doctoral dissertation of James G. Huneker. Of the seventy-two letters to and from Metraux covering the period 1943 January through 1944 May, there are seventeen letters from H.L. Mencken. Some other correspondents include Edward Ziegler, Guy Pene de Bois, Diana Huneker Lagen, Clayton Hamilton and Benjamin de Casseres. After working for the Red Cross, Mr. Metraux selected another subject for his dissertation in American Studies at Yale University. In 1949, he submitted a dissertation entitled, "Social Aspects of Swiss Immigration into the United States in the 19th Century," and received the Ph.D. degree from Yale. Before entering the Yale graduate school, Mr. Metraux received B.A. and M.A. degrees from the University of Michigan. At present, he is working for UNESCO in Paris as Secretary General of the International Commission for a Scientific and Cultural History of Mankind.
Mr. Metraux donated these four boxes of his papers to the Yale University Library in 1946 and 1947.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Delegate of the International Committee of the Red Cross, Delegation to the United States of America, 1944-1946; Yale Ph.D. 1949; Secretary General of the International Commission for a Scientific and Cultural History of Mankind, UNESCO, Paris (1969).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Ledger of the Company Koephfi & Suppiger and Act of Incorporation, photostat copies of German Original with English translation
Constitution and Colony Plan of Swiss Colonization Society, Cincinnati, Ohio
"La Coronella," a ballet by Silvestre Revueltas, Typescript of Episodes in Spanish and program of it, Ann Arbor, Michigan
Correspondence concerning his proposed topic for Ph.D. thesis, on James G. Huneker, at Yale University
Lists of papers donated to Yale University Removed to the donor files, 4-2-51
Outline of visits of the members of American Delegation of the International Red Cross to the P.O.W. camps in the United States
List of Branch Camps for the P.O.W.'s established before 1 Jan. 1945, but as of 1 Sept. 1945 not visited yet
Record book of visits to the P.O.W. Camps
U.S. War Department circulars and technical manual concerning German and Italian P.O.W.'s
U.S. Army Service Forces circulars concerning the German and Italian P.O.W.'s
Instructions of the International Red Cross to the members of the American Delegation with samples of report forms
Memorandum concerning the repatriation of the sick and wounded German P.O.W.'s to countries where they lived before
Material concerning dispersed families: notes of and address, reprint of an article, instructions and newspaper clippings
Basic food rations of P.O.W.'s based on the norm of Dr. Rossell
Report concerning the P.O.W. camps and activities of the American Delegation of the International Red Cross
Report on the activities of the members of the American Delegation
Minutes of meetings of members of the American Delegation
Samples of International Red Cross forms, stationary, labels, Christmas greetings
Confidential report concerning the situation of German and Austrian prisoners of war and war-disabled in the Autumn of 1946
Food rations for the German Service groups in the British Occupational Zone of Germany
Examples of Nazi propaganda; a calendar and a typescript concerning the onslaught of Bolshevism against Europe
Special edition of the newspaper Lagerstimme
Example of Menu, List of food on hand
Statistics of Prisoners, correspondence concerning the requests of the prisoners, English language study forms, copies of the newspaper Der Ruf
Status of Personnel at the camp
Library catalogue and a complaint of a German P.O.W.
Postcard view of J.S. General Hospital, memorandum, examples of camp newspapers, Lager Fackel and The Eurpoean

Two memoranda: from Metraux to the commanding officer, diagram of barracks, memo of food ration issue, order forms for food, clothing and equipment, blank questionnaire forms, itemized lists of things dold at canteen, various notices about camp activities, and an example of the newspaper Lager Rundschau
Memo from Metraux, diagram of the barracks

List of doctors, information about various P.O.W.'s, daily labor report, P.O.W. break-down, measurement and furnishings of barrack, inventory of food available, menu, entertainment program, photographs, newspaper, Der Aufbruch
Summary report on the camp, copy of camp newspaper, Deutsche Lagerzeitung
Examples of Catholic and Protestant Publications Der Katholik and Die Evgl. Kirche , camp newspaper, Die Lager Stimme,
Report on the camp by Metraux; plan of living quarters; sample menus; clothing issue; work schedules; report of Disposition Board, 20 and 21, June 1945; 2 photographs
Synopsis of the play, Blue Mask and camp newspaper, Der Aufbruch

Memorandum from Metraux to C.O., list of items sold by canteen, report on camp activities by German spokesman, menu, list of approved clothing, articles, sample of birthday card, photographs, camp newspaper, Die Lupe
Memo on church services for prisoners, example of staff newspaper, The Sand Storm
Report on German soldier killed in action, camp newspaper, Deutsche Soldatenzeitung

Memo to C.O., complaint by P.O.W. spokesman, response to memo of July 18, 1945, property to be retained by P.O.W., school schedule, menu, wages for work details, disposition of Co. 3, price list for the canteen, breakdown of P.O.W. personnel, daily disposition of P.O.W., breakdown of languages spoken by P.O.W.'s, list of inquiries left by P.O.W.'s for the delegate of the International Red Cross
Statistics of P.O.W.'s, sample of menu, lists of career handbooks in camp library, inventory of sport equipment, itemized price list of articles in canteen, menu for 20-21
Examples of camp newspaper, Lager Bote
Speech by Commandant Eley, report on visit by Metraux, roster of P.O.W.'s
Examples of camp newspapers, Amerikas Demokratie, Amerika Volkswirtschaft and Wirtschaftsleben, Amerika Formit Sich
Schedule for radio programs, camp newspapers, Der Aufbau and Bildung und Wissen

Memo to C.O., report on physical training and recreational activities, report on camp library, mental education and study aides for instruction, list of incoming mail, statistics of main stockade, sample form for the listing of all P.O.W.'s interned, list of deaths that occurred since last visit of the delegate, list of articles bought by P.O.W. Fund, amount of money spent by canteen, request for more adequate care of sick P.O.W.'s, samples of menus, camp newspaper, Neue Deutsche Woche
Memo to C.O., report on camp sports and theater statistics of P.W., outline of pay incentives for P.W.
Example of newspaper, Texas Bord Post
Report on the visit of Guy S. Metraux
Report on visit by Metraux and memo, report concerning the studies of P.O.W.'s, request for the return of personal belongings, list of stock on hand in the canteen, list of books in camp library
Example of camp newspaper, Der Deutsche

Report on visit of Metraux, memo to C.O., report on work detachments, special report Alfred L. Cardinaux, catalogue of books, music program, list of clothing and furnishings, outline of trade and study courses, photographs, camp newspaper, Der Neueweg
Example of camp newspaper, Deutsches Echo
Example of camp newspaper, Die Post
Price lists for prisoners, list of food allowance per man, photos
Example of camp newspaper, Der Herr sprichtzumir (a Christmas paper)
Example of camp newspaper, Die Brücke
Regulations for courses of study at the camp
Examples of camp newspaper, Grandi Magazzini , reading and writing manual
Example of camp newspaper, L'Ortica and a pledge to obey orders
Reports on the visits of Metraux 1945, 1947, list of internees from Peru who want their families brought to U.S., examples of newspapers, The Santa Fe Times
The history of its founding and purpose
Publications on the work of the committee for the P.O.W.'s
Miscellaneous American publications for the German P.O.W.'s
Sheet music for song, Lill Marlene by Mark David
Miscellaneous brochures concerning the P.O.W.'s, civilian internees and detainees
"Venice" oil painting by German P.O.W. Erich Gierzevski, Camp Fannin, Texas
"Rural Dwelling", oil painting by German P.O.W. G.J. Schramm, Camp Howe, Tex.
"Maxim Gorki Bunker of Sevastopol Coastal Fortifications," water color by German P.O.W. K. Ehlers, Camp Ashby, Va.
"Wendell L. Willkie," woodcut by Russian P.O.W. Gamov
Woodcuts of Adolph Hitler and a P.O.W. Christmas scene by unknown prisoners
Sketches of a girl and a boy playing a Balalaika by unknown Russian prisoners
Memorabilia: Armband of the International Red Cross, Austrian Flag and coat of arms, Polish coat of arms, Italian rank insignia
Tents and messhall, POW Branch Camp, Texas. Watercolor
Questionnarie… pour les visites de camps des prisoniers de guerre par les délégués du C.I.C.R.
Correspondence re: Japanese from Peru interned in the U.S.
Berwick y de Alba, El Duque de
Woolley, Leonard [i.e. Woolley, Sir Charles Leonard]
